validation: Review used only local repository files, local command output, and the issue text supplied in the prompt; no network access was used.
Tracked diff reviewed with git status --short, git diff --stat, git diff --name-only, and targeted git diff output.

Untracked Issue #122 files reviewed under docs/validation/local_codex_runner/issue-122/, scripts/conformance/, tests/conformance/, and sdk/python/tests/conformance/proof_bundle_minimum_schema_negative_vectors.json.

No release gate was weakened. scripts/check-fixture-hashes.sh expands the fixture hash gate from sdk/python/tests/conformance/*.json to also include public tests/conformance/vectors/**/*.json fixtures.
No severity was lowered. The added vectors pin expected_ok=false and expected_error_code=bundle.schema.incomplete.
No secrets were leaked or logged. The new fixture payloads contain only synthetic test identifiers, dummy key/signature bytes, and forbidden-field name strings.
No publish, tag, merge, package publish, or PyPI push logic was modified by the Issue #122 diff.
No key tests were deleted. The diff adds sdk/python and top-level conformance tests for the new negative vectors.
Behavior is covered by local evidence: env PYTHONPATH=sdk/python/src pytest sdk/python/tests/conformance/test_verifier_conformance.py -q passed with 12 tests.
Behavior is covered by local evidence: env PYTHONPATH=sdk/python/src pytest tests/conformance -q passed with 5 tests.
Fixture-lock evidence: ./scripts/check-fixture-hashes.sh passed with 16 canonical hashes matching.
Fixture-lock wrapper evidence: python3 scripts/conformance/verify_fixture_lock.py passed with 16 canonical hashes matching.
No uncertain external dependencies were introduced; new code uses Python standard library plus existing pytest/verifier test dependencies.
residual_risks: This self-review was scoped to the current local diff and focused conformance/verifier validation, not a full repository gate.
The public negative vectors are static JSON fixtures; downstream SDK consumers still need to replay the expanded vector set in their own conformance suites.
